contributions: A controlled comparison of pretraining objectives.
gaps_and_limitations: Pretraining compute is not held constant.

key_findings.evidence: +6.1 average accuracy points across five held-out domains
key_findings.statement: Contrastive beats supervised on zero-shot transfer.
The medical-scan confidence is poorly calibrated.

summaries.methods: Two equal-parameter models pretrained on the same corpus, evaluated zero-shot.
summaries.overall_summary: A controlled study of whether a contrastive objective lets a model generalise to unseen domains. The gains grow with the domain shift.
summaries.title: Contrastive Pretraining Improves Zero-Shot Cross-Domain Transfer

summaries.overall_summary: A short second summary so the golden snapshot covers the multi-paper merge.
summaries.title: A Second Paper To Prove The Merge
